summary: Front‑of‑board tape captures an all‑time great Nassau show with a monster Dark Star, scorching Shakedown Street and stellar St. Stephen, all in superb sonic detail.
review: The recording is a front‑of‑board capture using two Sony 54P microphones plus a Sennheiser 441, blended on a Nakamichi and recorded to cassette – the result is crystal‑clear and richly detailed. Musically the show is hailed as one of the band’s finest of the era, highlighted by an epic second set featuring a soaring Dark Star, fiery Shakedown Street, a powerful Miracle > Bertha > Good Lovin' run and a spectacular St. Stephen/Wharf Rat finish.

ai_rating.rationale: All ten reviews are five‑star, praising both the performance (standout Dark Star, Shakedown Street, Miracle trio) and the high‑quality front‑of‑board recording; the weighted average yields an almost perfect rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Front‑of‑board tape recorded with two Sony 54P mics and a Sennheiser 441, blended on a Nakamichi 550 and captured to a Sony TC‑158SD cassette deck – reviewers note crisp, clear sound with no reported flaws.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is solid and relaxed; the second set explodes with a scorching Shakedown Street opener, a high‑energy Miracle > Bertha > Good Lovin' trilogy, a massive Dark Star, and a seamless transition through Drums/Space into Wharf Rat, St. Stephen and an uplifting finish.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Fantastic guitar work throughout; highlighted on Dark Star and described as being "on a mission".
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass praised for adding the perfect vibe to Shakedown Street and driving the rhythm section.
band_member_comments.Drums: Drums after Dark Star were especially engaging, with the rhythm devils keeping the crowd locked in.
